title: Effect Persistence Tasks
principles: Use Effect v4 native KeyValueStore for ordinary persistence and one supplemental AtomicKeyValueStore service for atomic conditions, mutations, and TTL.
Keep the shared package provider-, channel-, OAuth-, workflow-, and messaging-agnostic; domain policy remains with Codex OAuth and the agent channel.
Preserve physical Redis keys, canonical encoded values, namespace prefixes, TTL behavior, leases, conflict outcomes, and stale-owner fencing.
The Upstash SDK, Lua, command syntax, response decoding, and provider failures belong only to the explicit provider adapter subpath.
Consumer Config owns environment-variable names and passes schema-decoded redacted options to the provider Layer.
Primary operations are flat named Effects with tagged error translation in pipe; runtime execution and Layer construction stay at executable and composition edges.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts derive every boundary type; raw JSON, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, and stringly operation states are forbidden.
Public persistence errors contain only stable operations and safe messages; provider causes, rejected transaction data, keys, values, and raw responses are discarded at the adapter boundary.
Helper sprawl is forbidden: do not add convenience claim/lock/replay/repository wrappers unless multiple concrete call sites remain clearer and the abstraction owns real policy.
No React, visible route, or operator dashboard is in scope; any frontend requires a separate SPEC following frontend-composition.md and Browser verification.
Do not log or retain keys, values, Redis arguments, credentials, OAuth subjects, phone numbers, message content, provider payloads, or protected URLs.
globalVerification: Effect language-service diagnostics are reviewed for every changed TypeScript file with no unresolved actionable hints

Root oxlint.config.ts, oxfmt.config.ts, tsconfig.base.json, and knip.json remain authoritative; no package-local lint/format config, weakened compiler option, broad ignore, eslint-disable, oxlint-disable, @ts-ignore, @ts-nocheck, unexplained @ts-expect-error, as any, or double assertion is added

Every provider Promise is captured by Effect.tryPromise; no Promise is floated/discarded, no domain operation is async, Effect execution remains at framework, script, test, or app composition edges, and provider/Schema/persistence failures are not hidden with orDie, ignore, untyped catchAll, or fallback success values

Static scans find no direct @upstash/redis import outside @bundjil/effect-persistence, no coordination through KeyValueStore.modify/get-then-set, and no raw JSON, process.env provider package reads, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, dynamic Lua, broad lint suppressions, or helper modules
Every new non-test helper/factory/mapper/adapter/service/Schema alias has multiple real call sites, owns a material boundary, or isolates a tested algorithm; otherwise it is inlined or removed
Compatibility tests prove exact physical keys, encoded values, TTL equivalence, namespace isolation, Codex profile fencing, refresh-lock ownership, and delivery replay transitions
No React/component/visible route files change; otherwise stop for a separate frontend SPEC and Browser verification
Isolated Preview proof precedes any Production rollout and registers no second webhook on the shared Sendblue account/line
Any approved Production rollout records clean-source deployment, protected-route checks, bounded Codex and handset proof, duplicate suppression, leak scans, and immutable rollback evidence

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, record distinct parent passes for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality/helper admission, and verification/evidence coverage. Add passes while any atomicity, compatibility, privacy, Layer composition, deployment, or documentation finding remains unresolved.

tasks.title: Implement the provider-neutral persistence package and adapters
tasks.description: Create @bundjil/effect-persistence with the canonical AtomicKeyValueStore transaction contract, coherent memory Layer, and explicit Upstash Layer that also provides Effect's native KeyValueStore.
implementationNotes: Scaffold the package with the repo's source export condition, build/typecheck/test scripts, strict tsconfig inheritance, Effect language-service support, and no app/framework dependency.

Define AtomicKeyValueStoreKey, Value, Ttl, Condition, Mutation, Transaction, Outcome, Operation, and AtomicKeyValueStoreError as canonical Effect Schemas/schema-derived types; TTL is a positive finite whole-millisecond Duration, and the Upstash subpath owns Schema.Redacted URL/token options plus a prefix that rejects Redis glob metacharacters.

Expose one AtomicKeyValueStore.transact operation and re-decode its canonical transaction Schema at the public operation boundary in memory and Upstash. Validate non-empty collections of at most 32 conditions and 32 mutations, unique condition keys, unique mutation keys, and positive finite integer millisecond TTL before state mutation or provider I/O.

Implement the public PersistenceMemory Layer backed by one SynchronizedRef state and Clock so native KeyValueStore and AtomicKeyValueStore observe the same values and expiries; every atomic transaction performs expiry cleanup, condition evaluation, and all mutations in one SynchronizedRef.modify snapshot.
Use Effect Array/HashSet or another justified Effect-native collection path for bounded duplicate-key validation instead of ad hoc mutable collection helpers.

Implement UpstashPersistenceLive(options), accepting schema-decoded redacted URL/token and prefix options; use one Layer.effectContext or one private backing-client Layer so a Layer build constructs exactly one telemetry-disabled, non-deserializing client and provides both services from it.

Implement prefix-scoped clear/size by decoding every SCAN page, de-duplicating keys with Effect HashSet, collecting before delete, and documenting weak consistency under concurrent writes; never use these maintenance operations for coordination.
Use one static provider transaction program with Schema-encoded or positional arguments. Never expose eval/client/Lua through public exports and never generate command source from user data.

Map provider and Schema failures to safe tagged errors without keys, values, arguments, payloads, credentials, rejected input, raw responses, or unknown causes; native KeyValueStoreError values omit optional key and cause fields.
Do not add claim/lock/replay/repository convenience services, generic helpers/common modules, pass-through wrappers, or env loading.
mandatoryVerification: Primary operations are flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs; typed errors are handled in outer pipe with catchTag/catchTags/mapError.
All boundary contracts are canonical Effect Schemas with derived types; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, switch/instanceof protocol branching, or trivial helpers.

Provider unknown is decoded exactly once, Redacted.value appears only at final client construction, Layers are not built inside operations, one Layer build allocates exactly one client/state, and package code contains no Effect.runPromise.

Every provider Promise is returned from Effect.tryPromise; no Promise is floated/discarded, no domain operation is async, no new Promise coordination bypasses Effect, and provider/Schema/persistence failures are not hidden with orDie, ignore, untyped catchAll, or fallback success values.
Audit every new non-test abstraction by symbol and record its concrete owner/admission reason; inline one-call wrappers and remove generic utils/helpers/common/repository/manager modules.

Root lint/format/TypeScript/Knip config remains unchanged except a narrowly necessary workspace registration; no local config, ignore expansion, suppression, unsafe assertion, or unresolved Effect language-service diagnostic is accepted.

No TSX, React, route, hook, visible text, browser dependency, or frontend state file changes; if one becomes necessary, stop for a separate frontend SPEC with primitive -> composite -> layout -> route, leaf-owned states/commands, and Browser proof.
Record at least three parent audit passes with concrete ownership/call-graph, Effect implementation/helper-admission, and verification evidence; add passes until every finding is resolved.

Contract tests cover absent/equal conflicts, all-or-nothing set/remove, expiry under controlled time, persistent set clearing prior TTL, expired-value observations, exact 32-entry bounds, service-boundary revalidation with no state/provider call for 33-entry or duplicate-key typed values, one-winner concurrency, stale-owner rejection, multi-key commits, one-snapshot memory transactions, shared native/atomic state, namespace isolation, malformed responses, and provider failures.

Mocked Upstash tests prove exact command arguments, complete prefix-scoped SCAN pagination, duplicate-key de-duplication, delete-after-collection clear behavior, exactly one client allocation per Layer build, no FLUSHDB/global DBSIZE/unscoped scan, and safe response decoding without live credentials or retained values.
bun run --filter @bundjil/effect-persistence check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/effect-persistence test
bun run --filter @bundjil/effect-persistence build
bun run fix
bun run check
bun run knip
bun run verification
git diff --check
completionCriteria: The package provides native KeyValueStore plus AtomicKeyValueStore through explicit memory and Upstash subpaths.

No consumer migration or compatibility shim is required to test the package contract, but this foundation commit is not independently mergeable or deployable before a production consumer migration passes.
The package README clearly distinguishes ordinary persistence from atomic coordination and documents the unstable Effect import path.

evidence: Ownership and call graph: the new package owns provider-neutral atomic contracts, coherent memory persistence, and an explicit Upstash adapter; root exports remain provider-neutral, provider internals remain unexported, both Layers provide native and atomic services over one backing store, and no consumer, app, route, React, OAuth, channel, or deployment source changed.

Effect quality and helper admission: canonical Schemas and tagged errors own every boundary; public transactions revalidate before mutation or provider I/O; memory commits through one SynchronizedRef.modify; Upstash uses one static positional transaction program; every non-test symbol has multiple call sites or owns a material boundary; the one-use Schema alias was removed; forbidden-pattern scans are clean in production source.

Verification and evidence: frozen install, package typecheck, 23 package tests, package build, root check, Knip, full seven-workspace verification, full build, static privacy/boundary scans, and whitespace checks passed; no actionable Effect language-service diagnostics remained.

tasks.title: Migrate Codex OAuth storage, refresh locking, and fenced commits
tasks.description: Compose Codex domain services over the shared native and atomic persistence capabilities while preserving encryption, revisions, locks, config ownership, scripts, and hosted proxy behavior.
implementationNotes: Keep Codex environment names, default namespace, profile schemas, storage keys, encryption, lock policy, profile-commit operations, observers, and domain errors in @bundjil/codex-oauth.
Replace the Codex-owned generic Upstash KeyValueStore adapter with @bundjil/effect-persistence/upstash and preserve KeyValueStore.toSchemaStore composition.
Express initial write, legacy replacement, revision-fenced replacement/refresh/reauthentication, lock acquire, and owner release through AtomicKeyValueStore.transact.
Keep profile encryption and canonical Schema encoding in the Codex operation before the generic transaction; generic errors map to existing Codex errors at the domain boundary.

Migrate apps/codex-proxy and trusted-local scripts to one shared Upstash Layer per composition. Remove old provider subpath exports instead of retaining pass-through compatibility aliases after all callers move.
Remove @upstash/redis from @bundjil/codex-oauth only after direct imports and provider client interfaces are gone.
Do not change OAuth protocol, profile versions, refresh timing, forced-401 recovery, filesystem mode, Vercel bindings, or Production data.
mandatoryVerification: Primary profile/read/write/lock/commit flows remain flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs with domain-tagged catchTag/catchTags/mapError handling in the outer pipe.
Canonical Codex and persistence Schemas are reused; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, provider leakage, or wrapper/helper sprawl.

Every migrated provider Promise remains captured by Effect.tryPromise; domain services return Effects and do not introduce async operations, floating Promises, local Effect execution, or orDie/ignore/untyped catchAll/fallback-success handling for expected failures.
Audit every new or moved non-test abstraction by symbol; remove pass-through compatibility exports and one-call wrappers instead of preserving stale ownership.
Root lint/format/TypeScript/Knip config is not weakened; no local config, broad ignore, suppression, unsafe assertion, or unresolved Effect language-service diagnostic is accepted.
No TSX, React, route rendering, hook, visible text, or browser bundle changes; any discovered UI need stops for a separate frontend SPEC and leaf-component/Browser verification.
Record at least three parent audit passes with concrete ownership/call-graph, Effect implementation/helper-admission, and verification evidence; add passes until every finding is resolved.

Tests prove encrypted profile round trips, exact storage keys/values, initial/legacy/revision commit parity, stale worker rejection, concurrent refresh winner, lock expiry/owner release, shared ordinary/atomic state, and sanitized observers/errors.
All trusted-local scripts and apps/codex-proxy compile against explicit package exports with no old Upstash adapter subpath imports.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test
bun run fix
bun run check
bun run knip
bun run verification
git diff --check
completionCriteria: Codex domain behavior and encrypted stored data remain compatible.
@bundjil/codex-oauth no longer owns generic Redis adaptation, Lua, scans, SDK interfaces, or direct Redis construction.
One persistence provider Layer supplies both native and atomic services to hosted live composition.

evidence: Ownership and call graph: Codex retains config, encrypted codecs, logical storage keys, revision and lock policy, observers, and domain errors; the shared package owns Redis construction, prefixing, scans, responses, and atomic commands; the old adapter subpath, SDK dependency, provider interfaces, Lua, and provider-mock suite were removed; proxy and scripts reuse one persistence Layer while filesystem mode and Vercel bindings remain unchanged.

Effect quality and helper admission: all profile and lock transitions are named linear Effects over canonical Schemas and AtomicKeyValueStore transactions; exhaustive Match selects commit conditions; encryption and Schema string codecs precede persistence; generic errors map at the Codex boundary; every retained helper has repeated call sites or owns a material codec, domain operation, or Layer boundary; the obsolete provider abstraction and mutable lock store were removed; forbidden-pattern scans are clean in changed production source.

Verification and evidence: 23 persistence tests, 103 Codex OAuth tests, 21 proxy tests, focused typechecks and builds, proxy smoke proof, root check, Knip, full verification, full seven-workspace build, static ownership and privacy scans, and whitespace checks passed with no actionable Effect language-service diagnostics.

tasks.title: Migrate agent delivery idempotency to the atomic persistence contract
tasks.description: Keep the app-owned delivery state machine while replacing direct Upstash calls with AtomicKeyValueStore transactions and preserving every external behavior.
implementationNotes: Keep SendblueReplayStore, message-handle/event-coordinate schemas, keyed digest derivation, claim IDs, complete/retryable/uncertain policy, and domain errors in apps/agent.
Move only generic prefixing, atomic condition/mutation execution, provider response decoding, Lua, and SDK construction into @bundjil/effect-persistence.
Derive the same logical key suffix and configure the adapter with the existing prefix so the final physical key is byte-for-byte unchanged and never double-prefixed.
Encode claimed and terminal records with the existing Effect Schema codec; do not introduce persistence DTOs or generic replay schemas.
Convert lease/retention durations through the canonical persistence TTL contract without changing effective expiry behavior.
Compose one provider Layer in sendblue/live.layer.ts and inject AtomicKeyValueStore into the replay domain Layer. Keep ManagedRuntime only at the Eve channel edge.
Remove @upstash/redis from @bundjil/agent only after no direct import remains.
Do not change webhook auth, routing, identity, HTTP statuses, Sendblue client behavior, shared-line provider state, model path, or Executor connection.
mandatoryVerification: Primary claim/transition/channel operations remain flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs with typed domain catchTag/catchTags/mapError translation in the outer pipe.
Canonical app and persistence Schemas are reused; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, direct SDK calls, or wrapper/helper sprawl.

Every remaining provider Promise is captured at the existing Effect client boundary; replay/domain services return Effects and introduce no async operations, floating Promises, local Effect execution, or orDie/ignore/untyped catchAll/fallback-success handling for expected failures.
Audit every new or moved non-test abstraction by symbol; keep Sendblue domain policy app-owned while removing one-call provider wrappers and stale SDK interfaces.
Root lint/format/TypeScript/Knip config is not weakened; no local config, broad ignore, suppression, unsafe assertion, or unresolved Effect language-service diagnostic is accepted.
No TSX, React, route rendering, hook, visible text, or browser bundle changes; any discovered UI need stops for a separate frontend SPEC and leaf-component/Browser verification.
Record at least three parent audit passes with concrete ownership/call-graph, Effect implementation/helper-admission, and verification evidence; add passes until every finding is resolved.

Compatibility fixtures prove exact physical keys, exact record strings, lease/retention expiry, one-winner concurrent claims, duplicate suppression, stale-owner fencing, retryable removal, uncertain retention, and unchanged route outcomes.
Agent tests use the shared coherent memory Layer or a narrowly justified domain memory Layer over AtomicKeyValueStore; no process-local production fallback exists.
bun run --filter @bundjil/effect-persistence test
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build
bun run fix
bun run check
bun run knip
bun run verification
git diff --check
completionCriteria: The agent has no direct Upstash SDK dependency or provider command implementation.
Delivery replay behavior and current Production storage remain backward compatible.
The shared package contains no Sendblue, Eve, message, phone, or delivery-policy terminology.

evidence: Ownership and call graph: replay keys, records, digests, claim IDs, and delivery policy remain app-owned; one shared adapter in sendblue live composition owns Redis construction, prefixing, commands, and failures; direct SDK, client interfaces, Lua, response decoders, and process-local production state were removed without channel, route, identity, webhook, model, or Executor changes.

Effect quality and helper admission: claim and transition operations are named linear Effects over canonical Schema strings, exhaustive Match, Duration TTLs, and atomic absent or equals transactions; historical prefixed claim and record keys remain byte-exact while validated logical suffixes prevent double prefixing; retained helpers own repeated construction or the compatibility boundary; forbidden-pattern scans are clean.

Verification and evidence: 23 persistence tests and 56 agent tests passed with focused typecheck and build; exact records, concurrency, expiry, retryable removal, uncertain retention, stale fencing, invalid prefixes, and route outcomes are covered; root check, Knip, full seven-workspace verification, static scans, and whitespace checks passed without actionable language-service diagnostics.

tasks.title: Verify isolated Preview and approved Production rollout
tasks.description: Prove the refactor against isolated Preview storage first, then promote from a clean pushed SHA only after rollout approval and retain bounded rollback-safe evidence.
implementationNotes: Use existing encrypted personal Vercel bindings and confirm personal scope. Do not access Tilt Legal resources.
Preview uses an isolated proof key/namespace and must not register a second receive webhook on the shared Sendblue account/line.
Prove protected route behavior, provider transaction apply/conflict/rollback, native/atomic shared visibility, ciphertext-only profile storage, and safe logs without recording values.
Before Production, record the current immutable agent/proxy rollback deployments, exact intended commit SHA, unchanged environment names, and prefix classifications without secrets.

Production proof uses one bounded handset turn and existing hosted Codex profile; inject no synthetic duplicate that could deliver a personal message unless the replay guard is proven before provider dispatch.
Correlate only sanitized counts/statuses across Sendblue, Vercel agent/proxy logs, Workflow events, and storage outcomes.
On any compatibility, refresh, duplicate, route, or leak failure, restore the immutable prior deployment and leave webhooks/variables unchanged.
mandatoryVerification: Preview deployment is clean-source, READY, protected, isolated, and has no shared-line provider webhook.

Direct HTTP checks record exact health/auth status codes, content types, and machine-readable response behavior for affected agent/proxy routes; no browser-visible route changed, so Browser screenshots are not applicable.
Preview proof records atomic applied/conflict/all-or-nothing behavior and native KeyValueStore readback using only safe booleans/counts.
Production promotion has explicit approval, clean pushed SHA, immutable rollback references, unchanged encrypted bindings/prefixes, and READY health.

Bounded Production evidence proves one inbound/turn/outbound, no duplicate dispatch/send in the window, one hosted Codex completion through the encrypted profile, zero Preview ingress, and no error/fatal/leak findings; isolated contract proof owns synthetic duplicate and forced-fencing cases.
No values, credentials, phone numbers, message content, OAuth subjects, protected URLs, provider bodies, transaction arguments, or execution IDs are retained.
No runtime source, lint config, TSX, React, route rendering, or browser bundle change is made in this proof task unless a concrete failed criterion first amends the SPEC and reopens focused checks.
Record at least three parent audit passes for rollout ownership, proof-command/evidence quality, and verification/rollback coverage; add passes until every finding is resolved.
bun run fix
bun run check
bun run knip
bun run verification
git diff --check
completionCriteria: Preview and approved Production evidence demonstrate behavior parity and rollback readiness.
The existing production data remains readable without migration or rewrite.
No provider routing, account, line, model, Executor toolkit, or OAuth protocol state changed.

tasks.title: Reconcile documentation and complete the final Effect audit
tasks.description: Update current architecture, package/app runbooks, SPEC evidence, and task status, then perform the mandatory repository-wide three-pass audit.
implementationNotes: Update root README/ARCHITECTURE, Effect/repo/testing architecture guides, package/app READMEs, Eve architecture, this SPEC/task ledger, and active execution plans affected by current ownership claims.
Preserve completed historical task evidence and mark superseded current architecture claims explicitly; do not rewrite history as though the package existed earlier.
Document native KeyValueStore versus AtomicKeyValueStore, config ownership, provider subpaths, key compatibility, replay-versus-conversation storage, privacy, monitoring, and rollback.
Run a helper-admission review over every new function, service, schema, file, export, and subpath; inline or remove abstractions without a concrete owner and present value.
Run stale-claim scans for old Codex-owned Upstash adapter paths, direct agent SDK ownership, KeyValueStore.modify coordination, old dependency locations, and pending rollout language.
mandatoryVerification: Audit pass 1 records package/app ownership, stable import direction, production/test/CLI call graphs, provider isolation, and unsupported paths.

Audit pass 2 records flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs, canonical Schemas/types, tagged errors, Config/Redacted boundaries, explicit Layers, Effect-native collections, no unsafe casts/DTOs/manual readers/raw JSON/direct env/helper sprawl/hidden expected failures, and clean Effect language-service hints.

Audit pass 3 records focused tests, full root verification, Preview/Production proof, compatibility fixtures, leak scans, rollback evidence, docs reconciliation, and deliberately skipped checks with reasons.
If any audit pass finds a gap, fix it and add another recorded pass rather than accepting the count alone.

A symbol-level helper-admission ledger records every new non-test abstraction and confirms that rejected one-call wrappers, pass-through services, stale compatibility exports, and generic helper modules were removed.

No TSX/React/route/hook/visible-text files changed. If that invariant fails, the task remains incomplete until a separate frontend SPEC proves primitive -> composite -> layout -> route, leaf-owned commands/states/skeletons/fallbacks, typography/accessibility, and desktop/mobile Browser evidence.
Root lint, formatter, TypeScript, and Knip authority files were not weakened; suppression/ignore/unsafe-assertion scans and Effect language-service diagnostics are clean.
bun run fix
bun run check
bun run knip
bun run check-types
bun run test
bun run verification
bun run build
git diff --check
This task ledger parses successfully and stale path/ownership scans return zero current matches outside preserved historical evidence
completionCriteria: Current documentation matches the implemented package, consumers, deployment, and rollback state.
Every task contains at least three accepted parent audit passes with concrete evidence and no unresolved finding.
The final worktree contains no dead exports, duplicate provider adapter, stale dependency, secret/content artifact, or uncommitted generated output.
